title: agrostis canina subsp. fascicularis
intro: Agrostis canina subsp. fascicularis, also known as red top bent grass, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the family Poaceae, native to Northern Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, Agrostis canina subsp. fascicularis has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, which are believed to have numerous health benefits, including the reduction of fever, pain relief, and wound healing. The plant's herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. Additionally, Agrostis canina subsp. fascicularis has been used in folk medicine to stimulate the immune system and promote overall well-being.
